question: Design Round-Robin Arbiter for Fair Request Arbitration
description:
Implement a round-robin arbiter granting one requestor per cycle with rotating priority.

' +
        '**Example:**
' +
        '```
request=111 → grants rotate: 0→1→2→0→1→2...
request=010 → always grants requestor 1
request=000 → grant_valid=0, pointer unchanged
```

' +
        '**Constraints:**
' +
        '- One-hot grant output
' +
        '- Maintain last_grant pointer for fairness
' +
        '- Fair share over time for continuous requestors
requirements: INPUTS: request vector (N bits, request[i]=1 means requestor i wants grant).
OUTPUTS: (1) grant vector (N bits, one-hot encoding of granted requestor), (2) grant_valid (1 if grant issued, 0 if no requests).
ONE-HOT GRANT: At most one grant bit is asserted per cycle. Use one-hot encoding (only grant[i]=1 for granted requestor i).
ROUND-ROBIN PRIORITY: Maintain last_grant pointer (register storing last granted index). On next grant, search for requests starting from (last_grant+1) with wrap-around.
POINTER UPDATE: On successful grant, update last_grant to granted index. If no grant (no requests), last_grant unchanged.
NO REQUESTS: When request vector is all zeros, grant_valid=0, grant vector all zeros, last_grant unchanged.
FAIRNESS: Over time, if all requestors continuously request, each gets grant in rotating order (fair share).
TEST CASE 1 - First Grant Among Sparse Req: Two request lines high. Expected: grant follows RR from last pointer.
TEST CASE 2 - RR Advance: Same req vector next cycle. Expected: grant advances to next requestor.
TEST CASE 3 - All Request First After Wrap: All req high with last at max. Expected: grant returns to index 0.
TEST CASE 4 - Three-Step Full Rotation: Continue all-one requests. Expected: grants walk through indices 1,2,3 in order.
TEST CASE 5 - No Requests: req=0. Expected: grant all zeros.
TEST CASE 6 - Single Requestor: One hot req. Expected: matching one-hot grant.
hints: Rotate request to place last_grant at LSB, priority encode, rotate back.
